title: Roxbury man arrested for allegedly assaulting child he drove to and from school
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Crime Fausto Abreu, 60, had active warrants out for his arrest on three counts of indecent assault and battery on a child under 14, police said.
A Boston man was arrested on Thursday for allegedly assaulting the child he would drive to and from school.
Fausto Abreu, 60, of Roxbury, was arrested on Thursday near 100 Warren St.

title: Flight diverted to Logan after woman allegedly attacks other passenger
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The woman was placed in handcuffs.
ShareSend this article to your social connections.
A woman allegedly attacked a passenger aboard a flight that was diverted to Boston Logan International Airport Thursday morning, police said.

title: Currently incarcerated Boston man pleads guilty to disinterring woman’s body in 2020
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The man’s three-year sentence for disinterring the woman’s body will be served concurrently with his current prison term.
Rinnyers Pena, 47, pleaded guilty Thursday to disinterring the body of Alenny Matos, 38, in January 2020.
Boston Police Department ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Watch the Haitian national team arrive in Quincy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: World Cup This will be Haiti's first World Cup in 52 years.
Haiti national team player Markhus Lacroix greets supporters as the team arrives at the Marriott Hotel in Quincy ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Erin Clark/The Boston Globe ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Does this truck owner have any recourse over a potentially major engine issue?
author: John Paul
bullets: How To John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, hears from a truck owner who just found an older service bulletin for an engine issue he may be experiencing now.
The Car Doctor hears from a truck owner who just found an older service bulletin for an engine issue he may be experiencing now.
Nissan Q.

title: Child hit and killed by train in Springfield
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News A little girl was found injured on train tracks near a residential neighborhood, police said.
By Carson Lyle June 11, 2026 Less than a minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.
A toddler was hit and killed by a train Wednesday evening in Springfield, police said.

title: Alvin Campbell Jr., AG’s brother, convicted on all but 1 count in rape trial
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The defendant, Attorney General Andrea Campbell’s brother, was found guilty Thursday afternoon on 21 out of 22 counts.
Alvin Campbell Jr.
enters the courtroom after a jury found him guilty on 21 out of 22 counts in his rape trial.

title: Boston is getting a new Chick-fil-A. Here’s where.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Food News The new store will be in South Station, the resturaunt said.
By Carson Lyle June 11, 2026 | 7:28 PM Less than a minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.
Bostonians and tourists alike could be eating more “chiken” starting next week, as a Chick-fil-A will be opening in South Station on Thursday.

title: A.J. Brown raves about playing with Drake Maye: ‘The talent speaks for itself’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots "He knows what he is talking about.

title: Man facing murder charges after 2 men killed in Fall River, including 1 stabbed with pitchfork
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime “The pitchfork was erected into the air, anchored into his head,” police said.
ShareSend this article to your social connections.

A Fall River man is facing murder charges in connection with the deaths of two men Wednesday night, after victims were fatally beaten with cobblestones and one was found with a pitchfork impaled in the back of his head, authorities said.
